title: Thats the new manga
wikitext:
[]: Thats the "new" manga?             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:33 pm Alright, if you haven't heard - Eva Manga 11 is out..I don't know if it just came out or not but I know its out now. If it is new then why is it that this is posted? ---Naughty boy, don't link to manga scans.---

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:37 pm It's been out in Japan for eons.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:38 pm Because that link is a scanlation. Viz's release of vol 11 is new.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:45 pm I see - I see..No linking, that's understandable.. Might you know when the 79th may come about?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:55 pm Stage 79? We never even got a properly labeled Stage 78. :P And no one knows when it'll come out. New stages show up when they do, and we hear about them very quickly through the grapevine. So you can't do anything but wait.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:58 pm I do hope to see the rest of EOE done Manga style sometime soon and Reichu, I hope your not mad at me.. We will have to just sit and wait...Just like everything else for Eva....2.0

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Dec 01, 2008 11:00 pm Better not frkn kill Asuka off in the manga. Does anyone recall the Mass Production Evas even being mentioned in the manga? Because there's no part where Misato and Huygo? discuss the extra parts or Gendo on the plane.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 11:48 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: Better not frkn kill Asuka off in the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Dec 02, 2008 12:29 pm the manga series is infamously slow to come out; volume 11 (well, the uncollected chapters that make it up) were released in Japan MANY years ago and really, anyone that really cared about them got the scanlation or import already not exactly earth-shattering that its coming out now

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 02, 2008 4:24 pm V wrote: volume 11 (well, the uncollected chapters that make it up) were released in Japan MANY years ago

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Dec 02, 2008 4:55 pm ...you mean as one volume, or when the initial "Stages" came out?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 02, 2008 5:15 pm Ah, ah. Constituent stages, then. The butt-end of Volume 11 came out about two years ago, and the Volume 10 tankouban (as a quick reference point) was published in 2006. "MANY years ago" is still a gross exaggeration. The manga was started "MANY years ago".

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Wed Dec 03, 2008 6:31 pm Huh-huh-huh...you said "butt." Of course, there is a new the-creator-actually-will-get-money-from-this-English-version of an Evangelion manga coming out in June, Dark Horse's The Shinji Ikari Raising Project . And don't forget, you can send in your fan mail and art to evangelion(at)darkhorse(dot)com. --C.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Dec 04, 2008 1:31 am Carl Horn wrote: And don't forget, you can send in your fan mail and art to evangelion(at)darkhorse(dot)com.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Dec 04, 2008 3:30 pm Carl Horn wrote: Of course, there is a new the-creator-actually-will-get-money-from-this-English-version of an Evangelion manga coming out in June, Dark Horse's The Shinji Ikari Raising Project .

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Thu Dec 04, 2008 7:45 pm NAveryW wrote: Carl Horn wrote: And don't forget, you can send in your fan mail and art to evangelion(at)darkhorse(dot)com.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Dec 05, 2008 3:42 pm Isn't it easier if we pop in where Sadamoto may be and ask directly if he is actually drawing it? (Address is quite wonderful hee-hee... from the Sensei's wonderful connections)

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Fri Dec 05, 2008 9:24 pm The Imperialist wrote: Isn't it easier if we pop in where Sadamoto may be and ask directly if he is actually drawing it? (Address is quite wonderful hee-hee... from the Sensei's wonderful connections)

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:10 pm *Gasps in admiration* Oh my. I would like to say in big bold letters, 'You lucky so and so', but I guess that lacks finesse. Is it then possible, to put pressure on Sadamoto? And also, as it takes so long, could he be actually working on it, just being very slow, or being a bloody perfectionist?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 4:48 pm Sadamoto does have the new movies to distract him at this time. I'm not quite sure how involved in the process he is (I can't really penetrate the interviews with him), but I know he's back at the animator's desk, at least.
summary:
[]: Thats the "new" manga?             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Dec 01, 2008 10:33 pm Alright, if you haven't heard - Eva Manga 11 is out..I don't know if it just came out or not but I know its out now. If it is new then why is it that this is posted? ---Naughty boy, don't link to manga scans.---

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008
